nodes.context: Bootlegging gang led by Charles Birger in southern Illinois during Prohibition.
Bootlegging gang led by the Shelton brothers, rivals and later enemies of the Birger Gang.
White supremacist organization that enforced Prohibition in southern Illinois, clashing with bootleggers.

edges.context: Birger initially joined forces with the Shelton Brothers in 1920 to bootleg.
By October 1926, the Birger and Shelton Gangs were in open conflict over bootlegging control.
Birger and the Shelton Brothers attacked Klan leaders in Herrin in April 1926, breaking the local Klan.
The Shelton Brothers joined Birger to attack the Klan, as the Klan opposed bootlegging.
